titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/185

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=47 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-12 13:45:36.123684+00 | translation moa=48 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-12 13:45:36.104592+00

div.p:
Alexander . dei gratia rex Scottorum . omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue . clericis ⁊
laicis  salutem . Sciant presentes ⁊ futuri me dedisse . ⁊ concessisse . ⁊ hac carta mea
confirmasse deo ⁊ ecclesie sancte Marie de Melros’ . ⁊ monachis ibidem deo seruientibus
totam terram de Drumteihsuiene . ⁊ de Alesburc’ . ⁊ de Auchnephur’ . quam Rogerus .
de Scalebroc’ . eis dedit per rectas diuisas suas contentas in carta ipsius Rogeri . ⁊ cum omnibus
ad predictas terras iuste pertinentibus . Tenend’ in liberam ⁊ puram ⁊ perpetuam elemosinam cum omnibus aisiamentis ⁊ iustis pertinentijs suis . Ita libere ⁊ quiete . plenarie ⁊ honorifice .
Sicut carta predicti Rogeri . ⁊ confirmatio Dunecani . filii Gilleberti . inde facte predictis mo
nachis  testantur . saluo seruitio meo . Testibus . Willelmo . episcopo sancti Andree . Willelmo . de Bosco .
cancellario . comite . Patricio . comite . Malcolmo . de fif’ . Willelmo . Cumin’ . comite . de Buchan’ . Alano . filio Rollandi . Constabulario . Philippo . de Mubray . Waltero . Olifard’ . Adam . filio . Gilleberti .
Ingelramo . de Baillol’ . Alexandro . uicecomite . de Striuelin . Waltero . de Lindes’ . Johanne .
de Maccuswel’ . Apud Edenb’ . secundo die Octobris .

Alexander, by the grace of God king of Scots, to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, greeting: Let present and future know that I have given, granted, and by this my charter confirmed to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God there, the whole land of 'Drumteihsuiene', and of 'Alesburc', and of 'Auchnephur', which Roger of Skelbrooke gave to them by their correct boundaries contained in the charter of that same Roger, and with everything justly pertaining to the aforesaid lands, to be held in free and pure and perpetual alms with all their easements and just pertinents, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably, as the charter of the aforesaid Roger, and the confirmation of Duncan son of Gilbert made thereupon for the aforesaid monks bear witness, saving my service. As witnesses: William, bishop of St Andrews; William del Bois, chancellor; Earl Patrick; Earl Malcolm of Fife; William Comyn, earl of Buchan; Alan son of Roland, constable; Philip de Mowbray; Walter Oliphant; Adam son of Gilbert; Ingram de Balliol; Alexander, sheriff of Stirling; Walter of Lindsay; John of Maxwell. At Edinburgh, on the second day of October.
